rulings.Case Title: GOOD HOPE URBANITE PREMIUM LINKED FUND, LLC VS. BRADDOCK ROAD, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, April 28, 2025, Line 1. PLAINTIFF GOOD HOPE URBANITE PREMIUM LINKED FUND, LLC.'s MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. Plaintiff Good Hope Urbanite Premium Linked Fund, LLC's motion for judgment on the pleadings is DENIED. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: LEM HOLDINGS LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL VS. MCINERNEY & DILLON, P.C. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, April 28, 2025, Line 2. DEFENDANTs SPRINGEL & FINK LLP, THOMAS JACK,and DARREN EBNER's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. Defendants Springel & Fink LLP, Thomas Jack, and Darren Ebner's unopposed Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement is GRANTED IN SUBSTANTIAL PART. Good cause appearing, the court finds the March 18, 2025, settlement between Moving Defendants and Ciaran Harty is in good faith within the meaning of Code of Civil procedure sections 877 and 876.6 and under the analysis set out in Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde Assocs. (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488. This order shall have the effect provided by Code of Civil procedure sections 877 and otherwise provided by law for an order under sections 877 and 876.6. Moving Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. (See Evidence Code section 452(d) [court records].) Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) "Although the existence of a document may be judicially noticeable, the truth of statements contained in the document and its proper interpretation are not subject to judicial notice if those matters are reasonably disputable." (Fremont Indem. Co. v. Fremont Gen. Corp. (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 97, 114.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT BLAISDELL ET AL VS. CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A PUBLIC ENTITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion To Compel Futher Responses To Plaintiff'S Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, Propounded On Defendant Lyft, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, April 28, 2025, Line 3. PLAINTIFFs ROBERT BLAISDELL, and PORTALES, DAVID's Motion To Compel Futher Responses To Plaintiff'S Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, Propounded On Defendant Lyft, Inc. Plaintiff Robert Blaisdell's motion to compel further responses to Request For Production, Set One, No. 11 is GRANTED. The request at issue is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP Section 2017.010.) Plaintiff has shown Defendant Lyft, Inc.'s responses are incomplete because the driver of the vehicle rented the vehicle from Hertz through the "Lyft Express Drive" program, but Lyft refuses to produce the Lyft-Hertz contract related to the program. Defendant Lyft, Inc. has not demonstrated that any objection has merit. The court orders Defendant Lyft, Inc. to provide further responses to requests for production Set One, No. 11. Plaintiff's meet and confer was reasonable. Defendant Lyft, Inc. must provide a verification. The agreement shall be produced subject to the parties' existing Stipulated Protective Order, which was entered by the Court on January 16, 2025. The court does not find Defendant Lyft, Inc acted with substantial justification. Defendant Lyft, Inc. shall pay $1,600 to Plaintiff in sanctions, payment no later than 20 court days from notice of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: MOR FLEISHER LEACH VS. DEEL US LLC, ALSO REFERRED TO AS DEEL, AND LETS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, April 28, 2025, Line 7. DEFENDANT DEEL US LLC DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Off calendar. Defendants' motion is an improperly stacked motion. Demurrers and Forum Non Conveniens (FNC) motions are distinct (the former is a pleading and the latter is amotion), governed by distinct legal standards, and adjudicated based on distinct records. The court will not adjudicate a combined, or stacked, motion. Code of Civil Procedure section 418.10(e) allows a party to make a FNC motion "and simultaneously answer, demur, or move to strike the complaint or cross-complaint." But this provision speaks to timing (simultaneous = "at the same time"), not the manner or substance of the filings. Just as a party cannot combine in one filing for an FNC motion and an answer, it cannot combine in one filing on a merged record an FNC motion and a demurrer. Response to complaint due May 15, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
